jira_task.summary: Development: Build New eCommerce Homepage using React js
jira_task.description:
Description
Develop and implement a new, high-performance homepage for the eCommerce site to improve user engagement and conversion rates. The homepage should serve as the primary entry point for customers, showcasing featured products, seasonal promotions, and clear navigation paths to key categories.
What to Change
Structure: Build the new layout using the existing design system to ensure consistency.
Key Sections: Implement the following sections:
Hero Section: High-impact banner with a primary call-to-action (CTA).
Featured Categories: Grid-based navigation for primary product collections.
Trending Products: A dynamic section displaying popular items.
Newsletter Signup: A footer-level or mid-page subscription component.
Navigation: Ensure seamless integration with the main site navigation and search functionality.
Acceptance Criteria
[ ] The homepage is fully responsive, maintaining a clean layout across desktop, tablet, and mobile devices.
[ ] All interactive elements (CTA buttons, carousels, navigation links) are fully functional.
[ ] The page loads within performance benchmarks (e.g., meeting established Core Web Vitals targets).
[ ] All assets (images, icons) are optimized for web delivery.
[ ] The page design matches the provided wireframes/mockups for branding, typography, and spacing.
[Image: embedded image][Image: image-20260629-065115.png]

knowledge_context.summary: Build a fully responsive eCommerce homepage featuring a hero banner with CTA, a grid of featured categories, a dynamic trending products carousel, and a newsletter signup component, while integrating the existing site header and footer and ensuring all interactive elements, assets, and performance metrics meet the provided wireframes and Core Web Vitals benchmarks.

task_intent.intent_summary: Implement a responsive eCommerce homepage with hero, featured categories, trending products, newsletter signup, and integrated navigation.

constraints: Responsive layout for desktop, tablet, and mobile
All interactive elements (CTA buttons, carousels, navigation links) must be functional
Page must meet Core Web Vitals performance targets
Images and icons must be web‑optimized
Design must match provided wireframes/mockups (branding, typography, spacing)
dependencies: Form handling library (e.g., Formik or React Hook Form) for newsletter signup
risks: Potential performance regressions if large images or heavy carousels are not optimized
Cross‑browser compatibility issues with flex/grid layouts
Integration with existing header/footer may introduce layout conflicts
knowledge_context.codebase_notes: Create new components under src/components/Homepage; reuse existing design system tokens; ensure CSS modules or styled‑components are scoped; add unit tests for interactive elements; update Storybook stories for new sections.
knowledge_context.backend_reason: All requested functionality is purely UI; no new API or data model changes are required.
knowledge_context.task_deliverables_summary: Site header / navigation + Site footer + Homepage / landing page + Hero section + Featured products section

technical_spec.title: KAN-8: Build New eCommerce Homepage
technical_spec.overview: Implement a fully responsive eCommerce homepage featuring a hero banner, featured categories grid, trending products carousel, newsletter signup, and integrated site header and footer. All interactive elements must function, assets must be optimized, and Core Web Vitals targets must be met.
acceptance_criteria: The homepage is fully responsive, maintaining a clean layout across desktop, tablet, and mobile devices.
All interactive elements (CTA buttons, carousels, navigation links) are fully functional.
The page loads within performance benchmarks (e.g., meeting established Core Web Vitals targets).
All assets (images, icons) are optimized for web delivery.
The page design matches the provided wireframes/mockups for branding, typography, and spacing.
technical_spec.rollout_notes:
1. Open the homepage on desktop, tablet, and mobile to verify responsive layout.
2. Click the hero CTA, navigate through featured categories, and interact with the trending products carousel to ensure all links and buttons work.
3. Submit the newsletter signup form and confirm the form submission flow.
4. Inspect performance using Chrome DevTools Lighthouse to confirm Core Web Vitals targets are met.
5. Verify that all images and icons are served in optimized formats (WebP/AVIF) and that file sizes are within acceptable limits.
6. Cross‑check the visual design against the provided wireframes for spacing, typography, and color consistency.

test_cases.title: Homepage Responsiveness Across Devices
preconditions: User has navigated to the homepage URL
steps: Open the homepage in a desktop viewport (1920x1080).
Verify that all sections (hero, featured categories, trending products, newsletter, footer) are visible and correctly aligned.
Resize the viewport to tablet size (768x1024).
Verify that the layout adapts (e.g., grid columns collapse, navigation collapses to hamburger).
Resize the viewport to mobile size (375x667).
Verify that the layout remains clean, navigation is accessible, and no horizontal scrolling occurs.
test_cases.expected: The homepage layout remains clean and functional across desktop, tablet, and mobile viewports, with no broken elements or overflow.

test_cases.title: Header Navigation Functionality
steps: Click on each primary navigation link in the header (e.g., Shop, About, Contact).
Verify that the correct page loads without errors.
Click on the search icon and enter a query.
Verify that search results appear and are relevant.
test_cases.expected: All header navigation links and search functionality navigate to the correct pages and display expected content.

test_cases.title: Footer Links and Contact Information
steps: Scroll to the footer.
Click on each footer link (e.g., Privacy Policy, Terms of Service, Help).
Verify that each link opens the correct page.
Verify that contact email and phone number are displayed and clickable (mailto: and tel:).
test_cases.expected: Footer links navigate correctly and contact information is present and functional.

test_cases.title: Hero Section CTA Button Functionality
steps: Locate the hero section CTA button.
Click the CTA button.
Verify that the user is redirected to the intended destination (e.g., featured product page).
test_cases.expected: Hero CTA button redirects to the correct page without errors.

test_cases.title: Featured Categories Grid Navigation
steps: Locate the featured categories grid.
Click on each category tile.
Verify that the category page loads and displays relevant products.
test_cases.expected: Each featured category tile navigates to the correct category page.

test_cases.title: Trending Products Carousel Interaction
steps: Locate the trending products carousel.
Click the next and previous navigation arrows.
Swipe left/right on touch devices.
Click on a product within the carousel.
Verify that the product detail page loads correctly.
test_cases.expected: Carousel navigation works smoothly and product links function as expected.

test_cases.title: Newsletter Signup Form Validation
steps: Locate the newsletter signup component.
Enter an invalid email address and submit.
Verify that an error message appears.
Enter a valid email address and submit.
Verify that a success message appears and the form clears.
test_cases.expected: Newsletter form validates input correctly and provides appropriate feedback.

test_cases.title: Page Performance Meets Core Web Vitals Targets
steps: Open the homepage in a fresh browser session.
Measure Core Web Vitals metrics (LCP, FID, CLS) using browser devtools or Lighthouse.
Record the values.
test_cases.expected: All Core Web Vitals metrics meet or exceed the established performance benchmarks.

test_cases.title: Asset Optimization Verification
steps: Inspect image and icon assets in the network tab.
Verify that images are served in modern formats (WebP/AVIF) where supported.
Check that image sizes are compressed and dimensions match the displayed size.
Verify that icons are SVG or optimized font icons.
test_cases.expected: All assets are optimized for web delivery with appropriate formats and sizes.

test_cases.title: Design Fidelity to Wireframes
steps: Open the homepage and the provided wireframe/mockup side by side.
Compare typography, spacing, color palette, and component layout.
Use visual regression tools to detect differences.
test_cases.expected: The rendered homepage matches the wireframe/mockup in branding, typography, spacing, and overall visual design.

pipelines.test_suite_name: KAN-8: New eCommerce Homepage Test Suite
